title: Alcohol Use Disorder Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Alcohol Use Disorder Support Groups 13 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Families Domestic Violence Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ALCOHOLICS ANONYMOUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) is an international fellowship of individuals who have had a problem with alcohol dependency. 12-Step support groups are held worldwide. AA is nonprofessional, self-supporting, and apolitical. Locator tool on website to find nearby meeting. Meetings are generally available both online and in-person. 475 Riverside Drive at W 120th Street 11th Floor New York, NY 10115 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Meeting times vary by location Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS RANCH VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups and Alcohol Anonymous meetings. 65920 SW 61st Street Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Must be a veteran Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 706-9062 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous), and classes (smoking cessation and anger management). 231 Lyon Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Support Groups: -Men's Narcotics Anonymous: Monday 7pm-8pm -Narcotics Anonymous: Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday/Sunday 7pm-8pm -Wellbriety: Wednesday 6pm-7pm -Gamblers Anonymous: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:30pm -Women for Sobriety: TBD Email (503) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE CHANCE CORVALLIS ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous). 456 SW Monroe Avenue Suite 108 Corvallis, OR 973335412 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies, call for current schedule Email (541) 224-2750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE CHANCE LEBANON ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous). 365 S Main Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday 9am-3:30pm Email (541) 248-2750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE THE CHURCH ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hosts the peer support group Women for Sobriety. 238 3rd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Friday noon Email (541) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals struggling with substance use or those in recovery. Services include screenings and referrals to treatment providers or community partners as needed. Collaborates with child welfare and local Treatment Court programs. Offers peer support for individuals in recovery, affected family members, and families involved in other Relief Nursery services. 1720 W 25th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children age 6 and younger who are enrolled in other Relief Nursery services Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm. Evening classes available. (541) 343-9706 114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY ROBIN JAQUA CHILD AND FAMILY CENTER ACCESSING SUCCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals struggling with substance use or those in recovery. Services include screenings and referrals to treatment providers or community partners as needed. Collaborates with child welfare and local Treatment Court programs. Offers peer support for individuals in recovery, affected family members, and families involved in other Relief Nursery services. 850 S 42nd Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Families with children age 6 and younger who are enrolled in other Relief Nursery services Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm. Evening classes available. (541) 343-9706 114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) meetings and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) classes. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans; all discharge types Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health and substance abuse assessments. Specialists provide consultations, referrals, support and resources to staff, and group therapy, such as Seeking Safety. All consultations and assessments are confidential, except when there's a risk of harm to oneself or others, or in cases of child, disabled person, or elder abuse. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing the impacts of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING HOPE CHURCH LIVING FREE MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Christian faith-based support groups and classes for individuals experiencing addiction to alcohol, drugs, food, or pornography, as well as for those dealing with anger, depression, grief, and divorce. Offers Beyond the Walls, a class for families with loved ones who are incarcerated. Child care is available for support groups and classes. Also provides a community meal. Worship services are offered but are not required for participation. 2711 NE Andresen Road Vancouver, WA 98661 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Community meal: Tuesday 5:30pm -Support groups: 7:20pm, support group times vary -Worship service: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:20pm Email (360) 944-3905 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support services to the LGBTQ2SIA+ community and allies of the Lower Columbia region. Services include a community center, education opportunities, mentorship, and monthly support groups for adults, youth, and families. Limited financial assistance is available as funds allow for gender-affirming costs, name changes, hormone treatments, and other expenses approved on a case-by-case basis. Financial assistance is limited to once per year, with a $200 limit. 171 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Community Center walk in hours: Tuesday 2pm-5pm, Thursday noon-4pm. Other hours vary by service. Email (503) 468-5155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAMARITAN HEALTH SERVICES SAMARITAN CENTER FOR HEALTH EDUCATION CENTER FOR HEALTH EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides health and wellness classes, support groups, CPR classes, and resources that focus on prevention and managing existing conditions. Also offers wellness consultations. 740 SW 9th Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 574-4952 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS RANCH VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS FORT KENNEDY VETERAN SUPPORT GROUPS GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER SAMARITAN HEALTH SERVICES CENTER FOR HEALTH EDUCATION
